### Episode brief (from Spotify RSS — delete this section when done)

Wall Street’s "Green" Secret is Out. 
What happens when the architects of the 2008 financial crisis trade mortgage bonds for fish farms? In this deep-dive exposé, we pull the thread on 8F Asset Management and Pure Salmon to reveal a global web of structural obfuscation, failed technology, and broken contracts. From the high-level boardrooms of Deutsche Bank to the pristine (but empty) 3D renderings sold to sovereign wealth funds, we trace how "organized irresponsibility" is being rebranded as environmental salvation.
Is the $120 million Brunei investment a visionary move for food security, or a masterclass in extracting management fees while the actual fish suffocate in concrete tanks? We break down the AquaMaof collapse, the $21.3 million Rasfish lawsuit, and why "Project Jonah" quietly downgraded from a global revolution to a simple trout farm. Don’t just watch the marketing—follow the capital.
